When starting with an automated insulin delivery system like Tandem Control-IQ technology, it's important to be aware of both general education points and specific education points for this system. The general education points covered in the Master Module on AID Systems include:

  • Use fewer carbohydrates to correct hypoglycemia.

  • Consider infusion set problems if blood glucose levels remain high for an extended period without a clear reason.

  • Trust the AID system and avoiding unnecessary manual interventions.

  • Timing meal boluses 15 minutes before meals.

  • Utilizing Exercise Mode 1-2 hours minutes before activity

  • Stopping insulin delivery if the pump is disconnected for more than 15 minutes.

  • Responding to alarms and being mindful of automatic updates on mobile phones

  • Seeking peer support for guidance and sharing experiences.

Now let's look at the specific education points for Tandem Control-IQ technology:

  • Using Sleep Activity every night can lead to better glycemic control. When planning to sleep for more than 5 hours, utilizing Sleep Activity is advisable. For short afternoon naps, Sleep Activity is not necessary.

  • It's best to activate Sleep Activity close to your actual sleep time and ideally not within 2 hours of your last meal. This ensures you receive autocorrect boluses if needed after an evening meal.

  • If you skip breakfast, it's acceptable to keep Sleep Activity on for a while, but it's not intended for use throughout the entire day. If you do have breakfast, it's recommended to disable Sleep Activity to allow for autocorrect boluses if necessary.

  • After a meal bolus, the pump may prompt you to adjust the bolus based on sensor glucose levels. Read these instructions carefully and consider whether it's appropriate for your situation.

  • While it's possible to override the bolus calculator, it's generally not recommended.

  • Utilize different basal profiles to optimize insulin release during activities like sports or illness.

  • In Glooko reports, the total daily insulin dose is divided into a percentage of basal and bolus insulin, and all manual and autocorrection boluses are added to the bolus percentage. In general, it’s recommended to aim for a balanced percentage of basal and bolus insulin, for example 40-60% basal insulin.

By following these education points, you can maximize the benefits of Tandem Control-IQ technology and achieve better diabetes management. It is normal to experience some trial and error in finding the right settings when you begin using this pump. Have confidence and consult with your diabetes nurse specialist for any necessary adjustments.

docs.title: #5 Sensor
docs.text:
The Tandem with Control-IQ technology can be used with the Dexcom G6 or Dexcom G7 sensor. Depending on your location, there is also a possibility to use the FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus or 3 Plus sensor. You can easily switch between sensors.

If you have doubts about the accuracy of your sensor reading or do not feel it reflects your condition, perform a finger prick test to confirm.

If a significant discrepancy, such as a more than 20% difference, exists and persists between the sensor and finger prick readings, even when the blood glucose is stable and within the target range, it is advisable to revert back to Manual Mode and or replace the sensor.

If you use a Dexcom sensor, you can first try to calibrate the sensor to improve the accuracy of your readings. It's important to note that if you calibrate the Dexcom sensor, it will need one calibration each day until a new sensor is applied.

docs.title: #6 Share
docs.text:
With automated insulin delivery systems, it is possible to share insulin and glucose data with healthcare providers and family members.

In the case of Tandem Control-IQ technology, healthcare providers can check pump reports via Glooko or Tandem Source.

People who use the Tandem t:slim mobile app can be followed remotely as the pump data from the Tandem t:slim mobile app is uploaded to the Tandem Cloud approximately once per hour.

Family members can remotely monitor glucose levels through the Dexcom Follow app, provided that the user uses a Dexcom sensor and also has the Dexcom G6 or G7 app installed.

While real-time monitoring may not be available with Tandem Control-IQ technology, the ability to share data still facilitates effective communication and collaboration between users, healthcare providers, and family members, leading to better diabetes management outcomes.

docs.title: 2.2 Indications
docs.text:
Tandem Control has both a CE-label and FDA-label for use in individuals with type 1 diabetes who are at least 6 years old. The FDA-label also allows use in children from 2 years old and in adults aged 18 years and older with insulin-dependent type 2 diabetes.

It is suitable for individuals with a weight of 25 to 140 kg (or 55 to 308 pounds) and a total daily insulin dose of 10 to 100 units. The updated Control-IQ+ algorithm is approved for individuals with a weight of 9 to 200 kg (or 20 to 441 pounds) and a total daily insulin dose of 5 to 200 units.

The system is approved for use with Novorapid, Novolog, Humalog or Lyumjev 100 U/ml insulins.

Please note that Tandem Control is not approved for use in pregnancy or in individuals requiring dialysis.

docs.title: 2.3 Software updates
docs.text:
The Tandem t slim X2 insulin pump is capable of receiving updates. As a user, you will receive notifications if an update is required. You can check the software version directly on your pump by navigating to "Options," then "My Pump," and selecting "Pump Info." Ensure that your t:slim pump has the latest software version. It should be something around version 7.8 to 7.10, depending on your location. If your software version is lower, it’s advisable to update the software on your pump if this is possible.

To perform an update, you need to download the Tandem Device Updater on your computer and have an update-ID. This update-ID will be emailed to you when an update is required. The update process usually takes around 10 minutes. It is important to disconnect the infusion set from your body and suspend all insulin delivery prior to using the Tandem Device Updater. Once the update is complete, your pump will show the Update Successful message.

In conclusion, the t:slim X2 with Control–IQ technology offers a powerful automated insulin delivery system. By leveraging the control algorithms, adjusting key parameters, and understanding the specific education points, users can experience improved glycemic control and gain more confidence in managing their diabetes.

It's important to remember that starting an AID system, like Tandem Control-IQ technology, requires a learning curve and individual adjustments. Trial and error will be part of the process to fine-tune the system according to personal needs and preferences. Don't get discouraged if there are initial challenges or setbacks. With time and perseverance, you'll become more proficient in utilizing the system effectively.

docs.title: 3. Linking components together (14.5 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this instructional video, where we will explore the process of linking the various components of Tandem Control-IQ technology. Let's dive in!

Tandem Control-IQ technology operates using the following steps:

  • Every 5 minutes, the Dexcom G6, G7 or FreeStyle Libre 2 or 3 Plus sensor measures your glycemia or blood glucose levels.

  • The sensor then transmits this glycaemia data to the Tandem t:slim X2 insulin pump. The algorithm on the pump calculates the appropriate insulin dose and automatically adjusts the insulin delivery accordingly.

  • The pump data can be sent to the Tandem t:slim mobile mobile app, that will automatically synchronize the data every hour to Tandem Source.

  • To access the pump data, the pump can also be manually uploaded to Tandem Source or Glooko, using an USB cable.

  • Additionally, if you’re using a Dexcom sensor, you can install the Dexcom G6 or G7 app on your mobile phone, and send your data to Dexcom Clarity and Dexcom Share. This allows family members and caregivers to monitor your glycaemia using the Dexcom Follow app.

In this video, we will guide you through the process of establishing these crucial links. Specifically, we will cover:

  1. How to link a Dexcom or FreeStyle Libre sensor to the Tandem t:slim X2 insulin pump

  2. How to Link the Tandem t:slim mobile mobile app to the Tandem t:slim X2 pump.

  3. How to create a Tandem Source account.

  4. How to make a Glooko account and upload the sensor and pump data.

  5. and how to use the Dexcom Follow app in combination with Tandem Control-IQ technology.

So, let's get started and learn how to establish these important connections within the Tandem Control-IQ technology AID system.

docs.title: 3.1 Linking a Dexcom or FreeStyle Libre sensor with the Tandem t:slim X2 Insulin Pump
docs.text:
With the Tandem t:slim X2 Insulin Pump, linking a sensor to the pump is a straightforward process. Here's how you can do it:

1. Navigate to the options menu on the pump and select "my CGM."

2. Choose the CGM type you want to use.

3. If you choose Dexcom G6, you’ll first have to link the transmitter. Choose "Enter Transmitter ID" and follow the prompts. The pump will ask you to confirm the transmitter ID twice.

This linking process is usually seamless and hassle-free. However, ensure that the old transmitter is not near the new transmitter; otherwise, Bluetooth may still detect the old transmitter. Keep them at least 6 meters (20 feet) apart to avoid any interference.

Once you have successfully linked the transmitter to the pump, you can proceed to start the sensor. Here's how:

  1. From the options menu, select "my CGM" again.

  2. Choose "Start Sensor" and follow the instructions.

  3. During this step, you will be prompted to enter the sensor code, which can be found on the protective foil of the sensor.

If you happen to misplace the sensor code, you can select "skip." In this case, the pump will ask you to calibrate the sensor after 2 hours, allowing you to still use the sensor. However, the pump will ask for a calibration every 24 hours so it's advisable not to lose your sensor code to avoid these extra steps.

If you have already started the sensor with the Dexcom G6 app, the pump will pick up the sensordata without asking for a sensorcode.

If you are using Dexcom G7 sensors, you can select Dexcom G7 from the CGM options. You will see an option to start the G7 sensor on the pump, and will be prompted to enter the Dexcom G7 pairing code twice. This pairing code can be found on the Dexcom G7 inserter.

If you’re using FreeStyle Libre 2 or 3 Plus sensors, you’ll need to start your sensor using the paired Tandem t:slim mobile app rather than your pump. To begin, tap Settings in the app's navigation bar and select CGM and then FreeStyle Libre 2 or 3 Plus. For iOS users, tap Start Scanning; Android users should ensure NFC is enabled in their phone’s settings. Then, hold the back of your Android phone or the top of your iPhone near the sensor until it vibrates or you hear an alert. The Tandem t:slim mobile app will display a Sensor Session Started screen. Tap Ok to proceed.

Once a sensor is started, you can follow the warmup period on the Tandem t:slim mobile app and on the pump.

By following these steps, you can successfully link a Dexcom G6, G7 or FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus sensor with the Tandem t:slim X2 Insulin Pump.

docs.title: 3.2 How to Link the Tandem t:slim mobile app to the Tandem t:slim X2 pump.
docs.text:
The Tandem t:slim mobile app serves as a companion to the t:slim X2 insulin pump. Please note that the app's availability and features may vary depending on your location. For instance, as of the latest update to this course, the ability to deliver an insulin bolus from your smartphone is only available in the United States.

Ensure that your smartphone is compatible with the Tandem t:slim mobile app. You can check this on the Tandem website. Using an unsupported phone may limit some features of the app.

After downloading the Tandem t:slim mobile app from your device’s Play Store or App Store, locate the app on your smartphone and open it. The app will request various permissions and prompt you to log in or register for a Tandem Source account. If you are 13 years or older, you can create a personal account or a caretaker account.

On your pump’s Home screen, tap OPTIONS, navigate to Device Settings, tap Bluetooth Settings, and toggle Mobile Connection on. The Pair Device option will appear—tap it, and a blue checkmark will generate a device pairing code.

On your smartphone, tap begin to start the pairing with the pump, choose the appropriate serial number of the pump, and enter the device pairing code in the app.

After you tap Pair with Pump on the app, you can tap OK to confirm on your pump.

Now you can tap Sync Pump Data in the Tandem t:slim mobile app, and the app will display your Dashboard and start showing pump data.

Keep in mind the following recommendations:

  • To ensure uninterrupted communication between your pump and the app, it's recommended to turn off battery optimization settings.

  • The t:connect Tandem t:slim mobile app must run in the background to transmit and receive data between your pump and the Tandem cloud.

  • If you wish to use the phone-bolus feature, you must enable security features on your smartphone, such as a screen lock, passcode, or face recognition.

  • Be sure to disable automating updates of the operating software of your phone. Before manually updating your phone's operating system, ensure that the Tandem t:slim mobile app is compatible with the new operating system version.

  • When updates for the Tandem t:slim mobile app become available on Google Play or the App Store, do not uninstall the app, but simply update the app.

  • After pairing and syncing your pump with the Tandem t:slim mobile app, you can expect about 15 megabytes of data usage every three days. Data usage may increase with poor cellular reception. To avoid using cellular data for uploads to your Tandem Source account, adjust the app settings to upload only on Wi-Fi by going to Settings > App > Data Control.

docs.title: 3.3 How to create a Tandem Source account.
docs.text:
The pump and sensor data can be shared with a parent, guardian or healthcare provider via the Tandem Source platform.

  • Users and parents or caregivers can create a Tandem Source account via source.tandemdiabetes.com/login.

  • Users of the Tandem t:slim mobile app create a Tandem Source account when they first sign into the app.

  • Healthcare providers can ask for a professional Tandem Source account via their Tandem representative.

  • Admin users of a clinic account can add other team members.

When you log in your Tandem Source account, you can find several menus on the left side: Home, Reports, Orders, My Pump and Upload Pump. In the top right corner you can access your Tandem account information and the help menu.

You can upload your pump data manually by connecting it with a USB cable to your computer, or you can use the Tandem t:slim mobile app that automatically synchronizes all pump data to the Tandem Cloud approximately once per hour.

Tandem Source offers 3 standard reports: the Overview Report, the Daily timeline and Pump settings. Via the account settings you can share reports with your healthcare provider.

docs.title: 3.4 How to create a Glooko account and upload the sensor and pump data
docs.text:
The pump and sensor data can be shared with the healthcare provider via Glooko.

We will describe how to do this as a user via Glooko Personal, and as a healthcare professional via Glooko for Professional Use,

docs.title: Glooko Personal:
docs.text:
1. As a user, you can visit my.glooko.com and choose "Create Account."

2. At a certain moment you will be able to enter a "proconnect code" that connects your account to your hospital.

3. Create a password for your account and keep it accessible for future logins, especially when you need to upload data from your pump.

When logging into Glooko, you will be prompted to indicate the devices you use. For Control-IQ technology, this will include the Tandem t:slim X2 pump.

Then you will need to download the Glooko Uploader software, which is available via the settings in the top right corner.

Once installed, you'll find a Glooko uploader icon on your desktop. If you open the Glooko uploader software and connect your Tandem pump to your computer using the provided USB cable, the pump data will be downloaded to your computer within seconds. When you open your Glooko account, you'll find the downloaded data available for viewing.

docs.title: Glooko for Healthcare Providers:
docs.text:
1. Healthcare providers or institutions can purchase a professional Glooko account directly from Glooko. You can then log in via my.glooko.com using the username and password provided by your hospital.

2. Upon logging in, you will find an overview of the latest data uploads and a separate tab displaying an overview of your patients. On the top right corner you will find the "proconnect code" of your hospital.

3. You can customize the patient list by sorting it according to your preferences or using flags to identify patients with high or low glycemic levels easily. You can add a new patient via the button in the top right corner and you can add their email address to invite them to Glooko.

To upload pump and sensor data, healthcare professionals can either acquire the Glooko uploader software, which requires one license per computer, or opt for the Glooko transmitter device.

Many clinics prefer the Glooko transmitter due to its rapid device uploading capability and the elimination of frequent updates. The Glooko transmitter is compatible with most diabetes devices. Despite only being plugged into an electrical socket, it swiftly transfers data to the Glooko cloud platform in mere seconds.

You will find the uploaded data in the assign devices tab. Here you can assign the data to the appropriate patient. If the device has been previously assigned to a specific patient, their name will be suggested.

By following these steps, you can manually read the Tandem t:slim X2 insulin pump data to Glooko, whether for personal use or as a healthcare provider, and conveniently access and manage the data. Additionally, healthcare providers can access patient reports without a Glooko Pro account if the patient shares their login credentials.

docs.title: 3.5 Dexcom G6 app and Dexcom Follow in combination with Tandem Control-IQ technology
docs.text:
If you're using a Dexcom sensor with Tandem Control-IQ technology, you can enable real-time tracking of your sensor data for family members or caregivers through the Dexcom G6 or G7 app. This app allows you to share your data with up to 10 caregivers, provided they download the Dexcom Follow app. Detailed setup instructions can be found in the module dedicated to the Dexcom G6 and G7.

It's important to note that some users have reported instances where the Tandem Pump loses connection more easily when using the Dexcom G6 or G7 app. If you experience this issue, it's recommended to discontinue using the Dexcom G6 or G7 app while using the dedicated Dexcom app.

Creating links between the different components of Tandem Control-IQ technology is an important step in setting up the automated insulin delivery system. While the process is generally smooth and straightforward, there might be instances where you encounter challenges or face technical difficulties. Remember, you don't have to go through this alone. If you experience any issues or need assistance, don't hesitate to reach out for support. You can join the Tandem Control-IQ technology Facebook support group or connect with other individuals who are using the system. Together, you can share experiences, seek guidance, and find solutions. Remember, resilience comes from seeking help and building a supportive community around you.

docs.title: 4. How the Tandem Control-IQ technology works (3.5 min)
4. How the Tandem Control-IQ technology works (3.5 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this video, where we will discuss how Tandem Control-IQ technology works.

The t:slim X2 insulin pump with Control-IQ technology offers two choices of operation: Automatic insulin delivery through Control-IQ technology and Manual pump, each providing distinct features and functions for diabetes management. Let's explore both modes in detail.

docs.title: Starting Control-IQ technology
docs.text:
To activate the Control-IQ technology, ensure your pump is connected to your sensor and that carbohydrates are enabled in your personal profile's bolus settings. Access the Control-IQ technology feature by selecting "My Pump" in the options menu, then choose "Control-IQ technology." Here, you will input your total daily dose of insulin and weight. The adjustable values align with the indication of Control-IQ and Control-IQ+.

Once Control-IQ technology is enabled, you will notice a gray rhombus and a blue "B" icon on the home screen. The colors of these icons will change based on basal insulin rate adjustments made by Control-IQ technology:

- If a predicted drop in glucose levels is detected, the basal insulin rate decreases, and an orange rhombus and orange "B" icon appear.

- In the event of a predicted hypoglycemic episode, the basal insulin is temporarily paused. The rhombus turns red, and the "B" becomes a red "0." The CGM graph displays a red zone. Basal insulin resumes if the predicted glucose level is expected to exceed 70 mg/dl or 3.9 mmol/l within the next 30 minutes.

- For predicted hyperglycemia, the basal insulin rate increases. The rhombus turns blue, and the "B" changes to white on a blue background.

- If a glucose level above 180 mg/dl or 10 mmol/l is anticipated, an automatic correction bolus is delivered, and an insulin drip symbol appears. The active bolus can be seen in the insulin-on-board status section.

Pressing the sensor chart button displays a 3-hour sensor chart. The sensor graph doesn’t provide specific information about basal insulin adjustments, but insulin and autocorrect boluses will be represented by blue icons at the bottom.

To monitor your current insulin delivery, you can press the status icon located at the top right of the home screen. The status screen will display your current basal rate and your last insulin bolus.

docs.title: Using Sleep & Exercise Activity
docs.text:
Via Activity under the options menu, you can explore three additional settings: Activity, Sleep, and Sleep schedules.

- Exercise: Activating this activity increases the treatment values to 140-160 mg/dl (7.8-8.9 mmol/l). A movement icon is displayed alongside the window on the home screen. You can manually start and stop the exercise activity or set specific duration.

- Sleep: By enabling Sleep Activity, the treatment values are adjusted to 112.5-120 mg/dl or 6.25-6.7 mmol/l. A "zzz" icon appears next to the window on the home screen. You can manually start or stop sleep mode or set specific sleep schedules. Two schedules can be configured for fixed days of the week, such as weekdays and weekends, ensuring they don't overlap.

docs.title: Adjusting Parameters
docs.text: In Tandem Control-IQ technology, you have the ability to customize the basal rate, carb ratio, and correction factor settings. To access these options, navigate to the Options menu on your Tandem pump and select "My Pump." From there, you can choose "Personal Profiles" to switch between different profiles or adjust the parameters within each profile within the "Timed Settings". This allows you to fine-tune your insulin delivery according to your specific needs and preferences.

docs.title: Giving an insulin bolus
docs.text: When delivering an insulin bolus, the process remains the same with or without Control-IQ technology enabled. However, it's generally recommended to follow the pump's bolus suggestions. At the end of a bolus, you can choose to convert it into an extended bolus. Pay close attention to the pump's bolus recommendations, as adjustments may be suggested based on your current glucose levels.

docs.title: Suspending insulin
docs.text: To temporarily stop the pump, like during showers or swimming, you can access the options menu and choose "Stop Insulin." You will receive an alarm indicating that insulin delivery is suspended, and you can set a delay of 15 to 60 minutes before receiving the alarm. Restarting basal insulin delivery can also be done via "Options".

docs.title: 4.2 Manual pump
docs.text:
In manual mode, you have full control over the basal insulin rate and bolus calculator settings. The instructions for this are covered in the module about the Tandem t:slim X2 insulin pump.

Throughout your Tandem Control-IQ technology journey, it's important to maintain open communication with your healthcare provider for personalized guidance and support. They will be able to help you adjust settings and make necessary changes based on your individual needs. It may require some trial and error during your consultations to find the optimal settings for your insulin delivery. By working together with your healthcare provider and staying resilient, you can effectively manage your diabetes and lead a fulfilling life. Stay empowered and prioritize your health and well-being.

docs.title: 5. Creating and interpreting reports (14.5 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this video on report creation and interpretation in Tandem Control-IQ technology.

We will discuss how to do this with Glooko, as at the time of writing this course update, the Tandem Source platform was not yet available in Europe.

In this video, we will explore the different types of reports available in Glooko, discuss how to interpret them, and provide some important do's and don'ts.

docs.title: 5.1 Types of Reports
docs.text:
In Glooko, you can find the key reports under the tabs Summary, Graphs, Insights and Devices

The summary tab is the first tab within any patient profile on Glooko. Here you have the patient's name, date of birth and their type of diabetes. You can also create pdf reports.

Below that we have the time selector. Here, using the drop down, you can select between a variety of time periods, from one day to 90 days, and custom time ranges. You can select between blood glucose data and CGM data using the selector on the right hand side. Using the arrows below you can navigate between different time periods in the increments selected, in the time drop down box above.

As we continue to scroll, you can see that we have the glucose data divided into time in range, time below range and time above range.

We can also see other metrics such as the glucose management indicator, the averages, as well as the highest and lowest blood glucose readings within this time period. As we continue to scroll, you'll see that we are presented with an AGP graph.

Here you can see the dark line indicating the median and the various shaded areas showing the statistical distribution.

As we look towards the right hand side of the summary tab you can see insulin data. Here we have the average total daily basal dose and bolus dose and also this percentage split between each. We can see the total daily dose on average as well as the percentage of overrides that this patient is doing using in this case their insulin pump. Overrides will not always be available as will some other metrics will be displayed based on the availability of the data in the devices that the patient is using.

For example, for users of Tandem Control-IQ technology, the Glooko summary report will also show these System Details, indicating the amount of time in automode and manual mode, and the time in Exercise and Sleep Activity

As we continue to scroll you can find dietary information for average carbs per day and the number of entries and below that we finally have the history section here we have all the events listed for the time period selected including food, blood glucose results, and any insulin use.

The overview tab within the graph section of a patient's profile is particularly flexible and allows for deep insights into the data that has been uploaded. First we can select the time range that we would like to review from the drop down including custom time ranges. After doing this we can review the graph below where we see glucose results color coded for those that are above within and below the target range including a black trend line. Below that we have carbohydrates which are represented as proportionally sized bars on the chart representing the amount of carbohydrates that are recorded for each day. Likewise we have proportionally sized bars for insulin which indicate the amount of insulin that's been administered on each day.

The great thing about the charts within Glooko is that they can be interacted with. So when we highlight a particular day, we can see the median glucose for that day. We can see the total carbs as well as the total units of insulin over the course of that day. If we want to see this information in a more detailed view, you can actually click on that specific day. That will then bring up a much more detailed chart, which will show the sensor results but this time they will be presented alongside the individual blood glucose values that were recorded. Beneath that we have the carbohydrate values that were recorded and when we hover on them we have the exact time that they were recorded. We have bolus insulin which is presented with proportional bars and when we hover over these we get more information such as this one here.

Beneath that we have the basal ray where you see the dotted line represents a temporary basal the solid line represents a scheduled basal and we might get gaps in that line such as we see here on the right hand side which is signified by the red dots which show that the basal was suspended during this time.

For users of Tandem Control-IQ technology, you can also see the automated basal insulin delivery and the System details.

For patients that are using insulin pumps the Insights tab can be particularly useful as it allows clinicians and patients to see how the events and features that are unique to an insulin pump are impacting the diabetes management. Here you can see that we start on the set and site change tab. Here we can see how frequently a patient is changing the set or site their pump is located. You can see for instance that this patient has changed their pump set after two days, they have changed it five times after three days, and they have changed it three times after four days. This allows the clinician and patient to see if the patient is sticking within the recommended guidelines and perhaps here where the patient changed it after two days there was an issue with the pump that they would then want to discuss with their clinician further.

As we scroll down you can see the date and time of each one of these events and the time since the last set or site change. You'll see on the right hand side we have each of the events lined up here down the black line. We can see glucose control for 12 hours prior to the event and for six hours after the event. This allows us to see if how the event has actually been handled has impacted glucose management. For instance inserting a cannula into a hard fatty lump. This may impact how insulin is absorbed by the body and might be something that clinician would want to explore further with a patient and offer an education opportunity.

The device tab within a patient's profile shows all of the devices that are contributing data to the report seen within the profile. On the left hand side, you can find all the devices listed, along with the serial number and the time the device was last synced with Glooko. If we select a device such as an insulin pump you'll see we have device specific information including the basal profiles. These are represented in a text format and graphically, showing the units per hour the insulin will be delivered and how this varies over a 24 hour period. As we continue to scroll you'll find other information such as the carb ratios, insulin sensitivity and the target blood glucose ranges, which the device uses to provide guidance for both correction and bolus doses.

As we scroll back to the top of the screen we can access device settings. Here we have general information such as the active insulin time, the units that the device is reporting in, as well as parameters such as the maximum bolus and maximum basal rate. Please note that you cannot change settings from here, but they will be recorded. Which is really useful, because if you decide to change settings in order to benefit patient outcomes, but this does not have the desired effect, and you wish to revert to settings that have previously been used, you can use the drop down box on the right hand side, to view settings from previous uploads. You'll see on the left we have an icon of a clock and again that is shown at the top of the screen. That indicates that the time and date on this device do not align with the time and date of the Glooko server. In this case you can see by the text on the right hand side that this device deviates by three minutes. It's important that all the devices that a patient is using are synchronized in order to ensure that the reports are of the highest quality and you can gain the best clinical insights from them for assisting a patient and informing clinical decision making.

When generating a PDF report in Glooko, you will also have access to the Weekly Overview alongside other reports.

This report allows you to compare multiple daily reports in detail, providing a faster and more efficient way to analyze the data compared to viewing them individually through the charts tab.

docs.title: 5.2 Interpreting Reports
docs.text:
When interpreting reports with Tandem Control-IQ technology, the primary focus lies on optimizing the basal insulin rate, correction factor, and carbohydrate ratio, as these are crucial adjustable parameters. However, it's important to recognize that there is always room for improvement in the user's interaction with the pump. This includes factors such as proper utilization of features like sleep mode and activity settings, adherence to bolus recommendations, and effectively managing infusion set changes. By continuously evaluating and refining these aspects, users can further enhance their experience and outcomes with Tandem Control-IQ technology.

Here's a roadmap for interpreting the reports:

1. Assess Glycemic Information: Begin by evaluating the TIR (Time in Range) and TBR (Time Below Range) over the past 2-4 weeks on the Summary Tab.

- Ensure that treatment goals are being met, with TIR above 70% and TBR below 4%.

- It is important to pay attention to the frequency of meal bolusing, which is also indicated in the Summary Tab of Glooko. On average, there should be around 3 to 4 boluses per day for meals. If you notice an unusually high number of boluses, such as 20 boluses per day, it may indicate that the user doesn’t trust the system and is constantly interfering with manual correction boluses.

2. Optimize AID Settings: Analyze the AGP (Ambulatory Glucose Profile) on the Summary Tab to identify trends of hypo- or hyperglycemia. Check if these trends are related to boluses on the Weekly Overview reports.

  • If you notice trends after meal boluses, it's important to assess various factors such as timing and accurate carbohydrate counting, before adjusting the carb ratio.

  • If you see a trend after manual or autocorrection boluses, you can try to adjust the correction factor.

  • And for trends outside of boluses, consider adjusting the basal insulin rate with 10-20% from 1 hour before the trend is noticed. Additionally, explore possible correlations with factors such as exercise, alcohol consumption, or hypoglycemia. Remember that with the Tandem Pump, it is possible to create a separate personal profile for special circumstances such as sports and illness.

In Tandem Control-IQ technology, it is important to ensure that the basal insulin rate is appropriately set. If you notice a consistent pattern of increasing or decreasing basal insulin during a specific period of the day, the aim is to adjust the basal insulin rate at that time to align with the algorithm's requirements. Keep in mind that it is generally recommended to aim for a basal/bolus ratio of around 40/60 or 60/40%.

3. Guide Behavioral Recommendations: To ensure the proper usage of the automated insulin delivery system, it is important to review various aspects in the reports available. Here are some key considerations:

  • Summary Tab: Check the sensor wear duration, time spent in auto mode, and adherence to the Sleep Activity. The sensor wear time and the time in automode should be more than 85%.

  • It is normal that the time in “manual mode” is not 0%, as the pump switches to manual mode during the sensor warm-up period or temporary connection loss with the pump. A high time in manual mode is mainly caused by connectivity issues between the sensor and the pump. In that case, recommend wearing the pump on the same side as the CGM and positioning the pump with the screen facing outwards. Keeping the software up to date can also help improve connectivity. Remember that by default, Control-IQ technology has an automatic switch-off feature that activates after 12 hours of inactivity on the pump. Consider disabling this automatic switch-off function of Control-IQ technology, if you think it causes a lower time in auto mode.

  • The time in Sleep Activity should be approximately 8 hours a day or around 30% of the time. Ensure that a sleep schedule is set consistently to support effective glucose management during sleep.

  • Also in the Summary Tab: Evaluate if bolus calculator overrides are low (ideally 0%). Also, check the amount of carbs entered for meals.

  • Weekly Overview Report: Assess the adherence to pre-meal bolusing, overcorrection of hypo- and hyperglycemia, and the appropriate use of Exercise and Sleep Activity.

  • On the Insights tab, you can check if the infusion set replacement is done every 3 days.

  • Devices Tab: Assess the appropriateness of alarm settings and reminders in the Tandem pump. Verify that the settings align with the user's needs and preferences.

4. Review Pump Settings and Emergency Plans:

  • Update weight and total daily dose of insulin at each check-up if necessary.

  • Document all pump settings and establish an emergency plan, including insulin pen usage, in case of a pump malfunction. Ensure access to insulin pens.

In conclusion, the accurate interpretation of reports and ongoing communication with your healthcare provider are essential for maximizing the benefits of Tandem Control-IQ technology. By effectively utilizing these tools and staying informed, you can enhance your diabetes management and lead a healthy lifestyle.

Additionally, we would like to share some valuable resources from the Barbara Davis Center in Colorado. These resources, available for free download on their website "panther diabetes," provide two roadmaps for monitoring individuals using Control-IQ technology. These roadmaps offer valuable guidance and ensure that important educational aspects are not overlooked. You can also find these resources at the bottom of the video under the downloads section.

Remember, with the right tools and support, you can optimize your experience with Tandem Control-IQ technology and achieve better control over your diabetes. Stay proactive and empowered in your journey towards optimal health and well-being.

docs.title: 6. Managing special situations (4.5 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this video on managing special situations with Tandem Control-IQ technology

In this video, we will provide specific tips for managing hypo- and hyperglycemia, fatty meals, sports, illness, alcohol and travel while using Tandem Control–IQ technology. These guidelines complement the general recommendations for managing these situations while wearing an automated insulin delivery system, which can be found in the general module on automated insulin delivery system.

docs.title: 6.1 Hypoglycemia
docs.text:
With Tandem Control-IQ technology, you may require fewer carbohydrates to treat or prevent hypoglycemia compared to when not using an AID system, especially if you have little insulin on board. You can check your insulin on board on the home screen and the exact amount of basal insulin you are currently receiving on the status screen.

If you consume less than 10 grams of carbohydrates to treat or prevent a hypo, there is no need to enter this in the pump.

To prevent hypoglycemia, you can use the Exercise Activity.

docs.title: 6.2 Hyperglycemia
docs.text:
Tandem Control-IQ technology is not a learning algorithm, so manual autocorrection boluses can be given in between. However, it is important to follow the bolus advice.

In cases of prolonged severe hyperglycemia, it is important to consider infusion problems.

To avoid infusion set problems, it is recommended to replace the infusion set in a timely manner and set a reminder for this on your pump.

docs.title: 6.3 High-fat meals
docs.text:
The Tandem Pump allows you to use an extended bolus. For fatty meals, it is generally recommended to deliver 50% of the bolus immediately and the remaining 50% over the next 2 hours.

This is the default setting for an extended bolus, but further individual adjustments may be needed.

docs.title: 6.4 Exercise
docs.text:
When exercising with Tandem Control-IQ technology, you can activate the Exercise Activity and reduce the meal bolus as discussed in the general module on automated insulin delivery systems.

It's important to note that autocorrection boluses are still given during Activity Mode.

You can also use a separate personal profile for sports during that time. This provides more options to adjust the insulin release according to your personal needs.

If you are using the updated Control-IQ+ software, you also have the option to set a dedicated temporary basal rate during exercise, providing additional flexibility to tailor insulin delivery to your individual needs.

docs.title: 6.5 Illness
docs.text:
During illness, hyperglycemia is common. Follow the guidelines for managing hyperglycemia in such situations.

It’s recommended to preset a personal profile dedicated to illness with a higher % of basal insulin rate and carb ratio.

If you are using the updated Control-IQ+ software, you also have the option to set a dedicated temporary basal rate during illness, providing additional flexibility to tailor insulin delivery to your individual needs.

docs.title: 6.6 Alcohol
docs.text: Refer to the specific guidelines for managing your automated insulin delivery in relation to alcohol consumption.

docs.title: 6.7 Travel
docs.text:
When traveling with the Tandem t:slim X2 pump, you have the option to request an additional insulin "loaner pump" to accompany you during your holiday. Moreover, in case of an emergency or if your pump requires replacement, Tandem can send you a replacement insulin pump promptly.

When travelling, be sure to also bring an extra Dexcom G6 transmitter if you’re using the Dexcom G6 sensor, and necessary charging equipment to keep your pump powered during the journey.

When travelling across time zones, you will need to manually adjust the time on your Tandem t:slim X2 insulin pump.

By following these tips, you can effectively manage various situations while using Tandem Control-IQ technology and maintain optimal control over your diabetes. Remember to consult with your healthcare provider for personalized guidance and support in managing specific scenarios.

docs.title: 7. Case report (2 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this concluding video featuring a case study on the Tandem Control-IQ technology.

First of all, Congratulations on completing this module on Tandem Control-IQ technology! By now, you have gained valuable insights into the features, functionalities, and best practices of this innovative advanced hybrid automated insulin delivery system.

To truly grasp the impact and effectiveness of Tandem Control-IQ technology, we highly recommend watching an insightful YouTube video review by Tom from TypeOneTalks. This video serves as a perfect recap of all the valuable lessons we've covered so far, illustrating how Control-IQ reduces the mental burden of diabetes management. Additionally, Tom discusses his experiences when he switches off Control-IQ, providing valuable insights. To access this review, simply click on the link below the video.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3VrWyXczDoA 11.5 min

We would like to express our appreciation for your commitment to learning about Tandem Control-IQ technology. By equipping yourself with knowledge and understanding, you are taking an active role in managing your diabetes effectively. Tandem Control-IQ technology has the potential to significantly enhance your quality of life, providing you with greater control and peace of mind in your day-to-day diabetes management.

Remember, always consult with your healthcare provider for personalized guidance and support on using Tandem Control-IQ technology. By staying informed, connecting with peers, and utilizing the resources available, you can confidently navigate the intricacies of diabetes management and make the most of the advanced features offered by Tandem Control-IQ technology.

Continue to embrace the possibilities that Tandem Control-IQ technology offers and empower yourself to lead a fulfilling life while effectively managing your diabetes. Together, we can make a positive difference in the lives of those living with diabetes.

Keep up the great work, and best wishes for your continued success on your diabetes journey!

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Diabetes Technology Expert Program

docs.title: Lessons
docs.text:
• 1. Introduction
  • 2. Tandem Mobi System’s Control IQ-technology according to the CARES Paradigm
  • 3. Linking components together
  • 4. How the Tandem Mobi Control-IQ technology works
  • 5. Creating and interpreting reports
  • 6. Managing special situations
  • 7. Case report

docs.title: 1. Introduction
docs.text:
Welcome to this module on Tandem Mobi System, the smallest durable automated insulin delivery system available in the U.S that can be fully controlled by a mobile device.

In this comprehensive guide, we will delve into the intricacies of the Tandem Mobi System, providing you with a step-by-step understanding of its functionality and how to effectively manage your diabetes with this system.

Tandem Mobi System seamlessly integrates three key components:

  • the Dexcom G6 or G7 sensor,

  • the Tandem Mobi insulin pump, controlled by the Tandem Mobi Mobile app on your iPhone

  • and the Control-IQ+ technology that resides on the Tandem Mobi pump.

All sensor and pump data is sent to Tandem Source, Tandem’s dedicated data visualization platform. People can choose to use the dedicated sensor app, in which case data is also being sent to the Dexcom Clarity Cloud. This allows for family and caregivers to follow the glucose sensor data of their loved ones.

In our general module on automated insulin delivery systems, you have already gained valuable knowledge on comparing automated insulin delivery systems using the CARES reference framework, selecting the right system for your needs, interpreting reports, and managing specific situations with automated insulin delivery systems in general.

In the upcoming videos, we will provide detailed insights specifically tailored to the Tandem Mobi System, ensuring you receive a comprehensive and structured education on getting started. It's essential to remember that these videos do not provide medical advice. Always consult with your healthcare provider before making any adjustments to your therapy.

The videos were produced by Dr. Inge Van Boxelaer, the founder of Diabetotech and an endocrinologist at the AZ St-Lucas hospital in Ghent in Belgium. The content was reviewed by Gary Scheiner, a Certified Diabetes Care and Education Specialist from Integrated Diabetes Services in the US. Here you can see their disclosures. Their expertise and personal experiences ensure that the information presented in the videos is comprehensive and unbiased.

By the end of this video series, you will have a comprehensive understanding of:

  • How Tandem Mobi System performs within the CARES paradigm,

  • Practical implementation on the Tandem Mobi insulin pump,

  • Creating and interpreting reports using Tandem Source,

  • And managing specific situations such as hypo and hyperglycemia, high-fat meals, exercise, illness, alcohol and travel. Lastly, some insights from a real user of the Tandem Mobi System.

Whether you are a caregiver seeking to better support individuals using Tandem Mobi System or a user of the system looking for a successful start, these videos will equip you with the knowledge, tips, and tricks to enhance your experience. Remember, your diabetes journey is unique, and Tandem Mobi System offers valuable tools to navigate it effectively.

Congratulations on taking the first step towards a better understanding of Tandem Mobi System. We wish you the best of luck as you continue on this educational journey!

docs.title: 2. Tandem Mobi System Control IQ-technology according to the CARES Paradigm (11 min)
2. Tandem Mobi System’s Control IQ-technology according to the CARES Paradigm (11 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this lesson about the Control-IQ-technology according to the CARES paradigm, that is used in the Tandem Mobi System.

Tandem Mobi System offers a range of features that make it an effective automated insulin delivery system for diabetes management. Let's explore some key points to further understand this system within the CARES paradigm, its indications and its software updates.

docs.title: 1. Tandem Mobi System According to the CARES paradigm
1.1 Calculate
docs.text:
Tandem Mobi System’s Control-IQ technology modulates preset basal insulin rates based on the predicted glucose levels over the next 30 minutes.

Additionally, hourly autocorrection boluses are administered at 60% of the standard dose when glucose levels are predicted to exceed 180 mg/dL or 10 mmol/L within the next 30 minutes.

The target range, or treatment values as they are called by Tandem, are set between 112.5-160 mg/dL (6.25 and 8.8 mmol/L), with the goal of achieving optimal glycemic control during the day.

During sleep mode, the treatment values are further refined to 112.5-120 mg/dL (6.25 and 6.7 mmol/l), promoting stable glucose levels without autocorrected boluses.

docs.title: 1.2 Adjust
docs.text:
Users can modify the following settings:

  • the basal insulin rate (different profiles are possible),

  • the carbohydrate ratio,

  • and the correction factor or insulin sensitivity.

With the updated Control-IQ+ software, users can even set temporary basal rates from 0% to 250% for up to 72 hours while continuing to benefit from automatic insulin adjustments and correction boluses from the Control-IQ+ algorithm.

In addition to these adjustments, Control-IQ technology offers different glucose targets or treatment values for sleep and exercise.

  • Sleep Activity: This setting aims for narrower treatment values of 112.5-120 mg/dL (or 6.25 and 6.7 mmol/l) during nighttime to maintain stable glucose levels without using autocorrected boluses.

  • Exercise Activity: This setting is designed for periods of increased physical activity. It sets higher treatment values of 140-160 mg/dL (or 7.8-8.9 mmol/L) to accommodate the metabolic demands of exercise. Additionally, during exercise activity, the pump does use autocorrected boluses to help manage glucose levels effectively.

During the initial set-up of the Tandem Mobi System, users will input their total daily insulin dose and weight. These values can be modified later to ensure accurate insulin delivery based on individual requirements.

While Tandem Mobi Control-IQ technology provides flexibility in adjusting various parameters, it's important to note that certain aspects remain fixed. The default treatment values are fixed at 112.5-160 mg/dL (or 6.25-8.9 mmol/L), the duration of insulin action is fixed at 5 hours, and the target value for correction boluses is fixed at 110 mg/dL (or 6.1 mmol/L).

docs.title: 1.3 Revert
docs.text:
The algorithm for automated insulin delivery is called Control-IQ technology, and is located on the Tandem Mobi pump itself.

In the event that there is no sensor data available for more than 20 minutes, the Control-IQ technology will stop and the pump will revert from automode to manual mode.

In manual mode, the pump will deliver insulin according to your Personal Profile settings.

Once CGM readings are available, automode or Control-IQ technology will automatically resume.

If you see the Out of Range icon on your Dashboard in the place where your sensor glucose reading normally shows, then your Tandem Mobi pump is not communicating with your CGM and sensor glucose readings will not show on your Dashboard.

  • When a sensor session is active, you may sometimes experience loss of communication for 10 minutes at a time. This is normal. If you see the Out of Range icon for more than 10 minutes, move your Tandem Mobi pump and CGM closer together and remove any obstructions. Wait 10 minutes and communication should be restored.

The transmission range from the CGM to the pump is up to 6 meters or 20 feet without obstruction. Wireless communication does not work well through water so the range is much less if you are in a pool, bathtub, or on a water bed, etc.

docs.title: 1.4 Educate
docs.text:
When starting with an automated insulin delivery system like the Tandem Mobi System, it's important to be aware of both general education points and specific education points for this system. The general education points covered in the Master Module on AID Systems include:

  • Use fewer carbohydrates to correct hypoglycemia.

  • Consider infusion set problems if blood glucose levels remain high for an extended period without a clear reason.

  • Trust the AID system and avoid unnecessary manual interventions.

  • Time meal boluses 15 minutes before meals.

  • Use Exercise Mode 90 minutes before activity

  • Stop insulin delivery if the pump is disconnected for more than 15 minutes.

  • Be mindful of automatic updates on mobile phones.

  • And seek peer support for guidance and sharing experiences.

Now let's look at the specific education points for the Tandem Mobi System with Control-IQ technology:

  • Using Sleep Activity every night can lead to better glycemic control. When planning to sleep for more than 5 hours, utilizing Sleep Activity is advisable. For short afternoon naps, Sleep Activity is not necessary.

  • It's best to activate Sleep Activity close to your actual sleep time and ideally not within 2 hours of your last meal. This ensures you receive autocorrection boluses if needed after an evening meal.

  • If you skip breakfast, it's acceptable to keep Sleep Activity on for a while, but it's not intended for use throughout the entire day. If you do have breakfast, it's recommended to disable Sleep Activity to allow for autocorrection boluses if necessary.

  • When delivering a bolus, the pump may prompt you to adjust the bolus based on sensor glucose levels. Read these instructions carefully and consider whether it's appropriate for your situation.

  • While it's possible to override the bolus calculator, it's generally not recommended.

  • Utilize different profiles to optimize insulin release during activities like sports or illness.

  • When adjusting parameters, keep in mind that the correction factor functions as a lever to adjust the aggressiveness of the system, and is the most influential parameter to adjust.

By following these education points, you can maximize the benefits of the Tandem Mobi System and achieve better diabetes management. It is normal to experience some trial and error in finding the right settings when you begin using this pump. Have confidence and consult with your healthcare provider for any necessary adjustments.

docs.title: 1.5 Sensor
docs.text: The accuracy of sensor values is vital for the proper functioning of an automated insulin delivery system. If you have doubts about the accuracy of your sensor reading, it is recommended to perform a finger prick blood glucose test for confirmation. If there is a significant discrepancy between the sensor glucose reading and your finger prick result, you may consider calibrating the Dexcom G6 or G7 sensor. However, it is important to perform calibration only when your blood glucose levels have been stable and within your target range for the past few minutes. Calibrating under these conditions helps ensure more accurate sensor readings and enhances the overall performance of the automated insulin delivery system.

docs.title: 1.6 Share
docs.text:
With automated insulin delivery systems, it is possible to share insulin and glucose data with healthcare providers and family members.

In the case of the Tandem Mobi system, all glucose and insulin data are automatically synchronized to the Tandem Source platform.

Although there is no dedicated follow app of the Tandem Mobi system available, family members or caregivers can request access to Tandem Source by creating a free Parent/Guardian Use account.

Family members or caregivers can also remotely monitor glucose levels through the Dexcom Follow app, provided that the user also has the Dexcom G6 or G7 app installed.

For healthcare providers, it is also possible to follow users in real-time by logging into Tandem Source with a hospital account.

While real-time monitoring may not be available with Tandem Control-IQ technology, the ability to share data still facilitates effective communication and collaboration between users, healthcare providers, and family members, leading to better diabetes management outcomes.

docs.title: 2. Indications
docs.text:
The Tandem Mobi pump is only available in the US at the moment, but will come to Europe in 2026.

Control-IQ technology has both a CE-label and FDA-label for use in individuals with type 1 diabetes who are at least 6 years old. The FDA-label also allows use in children from 2 years old and in adults aged 18 years and older with insulin-dependent type 2 diabetes.

It is suitable for individuals with a weight of 25 to 140 kg (or 55 to 308 pounds) and a ~~minimum~~ total daily insulin dose of 10 to 100 units. The updated Control-IQ+ algorithm is approved for individuals with a weight of 9 to 200 kg (or 20 to 441 pounds) and a total daily insulin dose of 5 to 200 units.

The system is approved for use with Novolog or Novorapid and Humalog 100 U/ml insulins.

Please note that Tandem Control is not approved for use in pregnancy or in individuals requiring dialysis.

docs.title: 3. Software updates
docs.text:
The Tandem Mobi insulin pump is capable of receiving updates. Tandem will notify users by communication channels such as emails and website pages when pump software updates are available.

When updates to the Tandem Mobi mobile app are available from the App Store, do not uninstall the app. When you download and install an update, your Tandem Mobi mobile app will still be connected to your Tandem Source account, the smartphone will still be paired with your pump, and your app settings will remain the same.

You can check the software update on your Tandem Mobi App Account by navigating to the "Settings", then "Pump," and select "Pump Software Update". If there is a new update available, you can follow the on-screen steps on your Tandem Mobi mobile app to complete the pump software update.

Sometimes your iPhone will propose you to update your operating system. Before you manually update your phone operating system, confirm that the Tandem Mobi mobile app is compatible with the new operating system. For more information about managing automatic updates access the Settings screen from the Tandem Mobi mobile app. Tap Help, then Pump and App Guide, and then choose Smartphone Setup from the index.

In conclusion, the Tandem Mobi Control–IQ technology offers a powerful automated insulin delivery system. By leveraging the control algorithms, adjusting key parameters, and understanding the specific education points, users can experience improved glycemic control and gain more confidence in managing their diabetes.

It's important to remember that starting an AID system, like the Tandem Mobi system, requires a learning curve and individual adjustments. Trial and error will be part of the process to fine-tune the system according to personal needs and preferences. Don't get discouraged if there are initial challenges or setbacks. With time and perseverance, you'll become more proficient in utilizing the system effectively.

docs.title: 3. Linking components together (6 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this instructional video, where we will explore the process of linking the various components of the Tandem Mobi System. Let's dive in!

The Tandem Mobi System operates using the following steps:

  • Every 5 minutes, the Dexcom G6 or G7 sensor measures your glycemia or blood glucose levels.

  • The sensor transmits this glycemia data to the Tandem Mobi insulin pump. The algorithm on the pump calculates the appropriate insulin dose and automatically adjusts the insulin delivery accordingly.

  • The Tandem Mobi pump can be controlled via the Tandem Mobi mobile app on your phone.

  • The glucose and pump data are automatically synced to the Tandem Source platform via WIFI.

  • Additionally, by installing the Dexcom G6 or G7 app on your mobile phone, you can conveniently send your data to Dexcom Clarity and Dexcom Share. This allows caregivers to monitor your glycemia using the Dexcom Follow app.

In this video, we will guide you through the process of establishing these crucial links. Specifically, we will cover:

  1. How to link the Tandem Mobi pump to the Tandem Mobi Mobile app

  2. How to link a Dexcom G6 sensor to the Tandem Mobi insulin pump

  3. How to link a Dexcom G7 sensor to the Tandem Mobi insulin pump

  4. How to create a Tandem Source account.

So, let's get started and learn how to establish these important connections within the Tandem Mobi System.

docs.title: 3.1 How to link the Tandem Mobi pump to the Tandem Mobi Mobile app
docs.text:
In order to use your Mobi pump, you must first pair your pump to your iPhone. You can do as follows:

  • Connect your charging pad to a power source using the supplied USB-C cable and place the pump on the charging pad.

  • Ensure that your pump is turned on. You can check to see if your pump is on by pressing the Pump button once. If the pump status lights turn on, your pump is on.

  • Download the Tandem Mobi Mobile app from the App store on your compatible iPhone.

  • If you are a new user, the app will first ask you to create an account on Tandem Source.

  • Next tap "Begin", and follow the on-screen instructions to pair your Mobi pump.

  • At a certain moment, you will be asked to enter the PIN code of your pump, which can be found under the serial number.

When your pump is paired, you will see the homescreen of the Tandem Mobi mobile app.

docs.title: 3.2 How to link a Dexcom G6 sensor to the Tandem Mobi insulin pump
docs.text:
With the Tandem Mobi Insulin Pump, linking the sensor to the pump is a straightforward process, but it’s a little different for Dexcom G6 and Dexcom G7. Here's how you can do it for the Dexcom G6 sensor:

First link the Dexcom G6 transmitter by navigating to the Settings menu on the pump, tap CGM, and then Dexcom G6. Tap the Transmitter ID field and enter the unique transmitter ID. You will be asked to verify it, after which you can save it.

Once you have successfully linked the transmitter to the pump, you can proceed to start the sensor. Here's how:

  1. From the Dashboard screen, select "Settings," then “CGM”

  2. Choose "Start Sensor" and follow the instructions.

  3. During this step, you will be prompted to enter the sensor code, which can be found on the protective foil of the sensor. If you happen to misplace the sensor code, you can select "skip." In this case, the pump will ask you to calibrate the sensor after 2 hours, allowing you to still use the sensor. However, the pump will ask for a calibration every 24 hours so it's advisable not to lose your sensor code to avoid these extra steps.

  4. On the Start Sensor screen, tap Start. The Sensor session started banner will be displayed at the top of the Tandem Mobi mobile app to let you know your 2-hour warmup time has begun. During the sensor startup period, Control-IQ technology will not affect the basal rates or deliver automatic correction boluses. The sensor must be actively providing readings for Control-IQ technology to operate.

If you have already started the sensor with the Dexcom G6 app, the pump will pick up the sensordata without asking for a sensorcode.

By following these steps, you can successfully link the Dexcom G6 transmitter and start the Dexcom G6 sensor with the Tandem Mobi Insulin Pump.

docs.title: 3.3 How to link a Dexcom G7 sensor to the Tandem Mobi insulin pump
docs.text:
If you have access to Dexcom G7 sensors, the linking process is even easier. Just choose Dexcom G7 as your sensor type, and enter the G7 pairing code twice.

The Dexcom G7 sensor needs a 30-minute startup period to adjust to being under your skin. This startup period begins automatically as soon as the sensor is inserted. So if you insert your Dexcom G7 sensor 30 minutes before pairing it with your pump, you will directly receive sensor values, and Control-IQ technology will not need to stop when switching sensors.

docs.title: 3.4 How to create a Tandem Source account
docs.text:
The pump and sensor data can be shared with a parent, guardian or healthcare provider via Tandem Source platform.

Users of the Tandem Mobi Mobile app create a Tandem Source account when they first sign into the app. All sensor and pump data are automatically synchronized to the Tandem Cloud approximately once per hour.

Healthcare providers can ask for a professional Tandem Source account via their Tandem representative. Admin users of a clinic account can add other team members.

Users and healthcare providers can both login to Tandem Source via sso.tandemdiabetes.com. Users can connect to a clinic by going to their account settings, and then tap Share Reports, and then Add Clinic.

While the process is generally smooth and straightforward, there might be instances where you encounter challenges or face technical difficulties. Remember, you don't have to go through this alone. If you experience any issues or need assistance, don't hesitate to reach out for support. You can join the Tandem Facebook support group or connect with other individuals who are using the system.

Together, you can share experiences, seek guidance, and find solutions. Keep in mind, resilience comes from seeking help and building a supportive community around you.

docs.title: 4. How the Tandem Mobi Control-IQ technology works (3 min)
4. How the Tandem Mobi Control-IQ technology works (3 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this video, where we will discuss how the Tandem Mobi System works.

We will explain how to use the Tandem Mobi System in two modes: with Control-IQ technology on, also known as automode, and with Control-IQ technology off, also known as manual mode.

docs.title: 4.1 Tandem Mobi System with Control-IQ technology
Starting Control-IQ technology
docs.text: Access the Control-IQ technology feature from the Navigation bar, tap “Settings”, tap “Pump” then tap “Control-IQ”. To turn Control-IQ technology on, tap the toggle next to Control-IQ. Here, you need to input your weight and total daily dose of insulin. The adjustable values align with the indication of Control-IQ+ technology.

docs.title: Using Sleep & Exercise Activity
docs.text:
Under the Actions menu, you can explore three additional settings: Activity, Sleep, and Sleep schedules.

- Exercise: Activating this activity increases the treatment values to 140-160 mg/dl (or 7.8-8.9 mmol/l). A movement icon is displayed alongside the window on the home screen. Please note that Exercise Activity cannot be scheduled, and can only be activated or deactivated on the go.

- Sleep: By enabling Sleep Activity, the treatment values are adjusted to 112.5-120 mg/dl or 6.25-6.7 mmol/l. A "zzz" icon appears next to the window on the home screen. You can manually toggle sleep mode on or off or set specific sleep schedules. Two schedules can be configured for fixed days of the week, such as weekdays and weekends, ensuring they don't overlap.

docs.title: Adjusting Parameters
docs.text: In the Tandem Mobi System with Control-IQ technology, you have the ability to customize the basal rate, carb ratio, and correction factor settings. To access these options, navigate to the Settings on your Tandem Mobi App and select "Pump." From there, you can choose "Personal Profiles" to switch between different profiles or adjust the parameters within each profile within the "Time Settings". This allows you to fine-tune your insulin delivery according to your specific needs and preferences.

docs.title: Giving an insulin bolus
docs.text: When delivering an insulin bolus, the process remains the same with or without Control-IQ technology enabled. It's generally recommended to follow the pump's bolus suggestions. At the end of a bolus, you can choose to convert it into an extended bolus. Pay close attention to the pump's bolus recommendations, as adjustments may be suggested based on your current glucose levels.

docs.title: Suspending insulin
docs.text: To temporarily stop the pump, like during showers or swimming, you can access the “Actions” in the navigation bar and tap "Stop Insulin." Restarting basal insulin delivery can also be done via "Actions".

docs.title: 4.2 Tandem Mobi System without Control-IQ technology (manual mode)
docs.text:
If you want to use the Tandem Mobi pump without automated adjustment of the basal insulin delivery, you can toggle off the Control-IQ technology via the pump settings. The pump will deliver basal and bolus insulin according to your Personal profile settings. There is no suspend-before-low option available if you disable Control-IQ technology.

Throughout your Tandem Mobi system journey, it's important to maintain open communication with your healthcare provider for personalized guidance and support. They will be able to help you adjust settings and make necessary changes based on your individual needs. It may require some trial and error during your consultations to find the optimal settings for your insulin delivery.

By working together with your healthcare provider and staying resilient, you can effectively manage your diabetes and lead a fulfilling life. Stay empowered and prioritize your health and well-being.

docs.title: 5. Creating and interpreting reports (7 min)
docs.text:
Greetings and welcome to this video on Tandem Mobi System report creation and interpretation.

We will discuss how to do this with the Tandem Source Platform.

In this video, we will explore the types of reports available in theTandem Source Platform, and discuss how to interpret them.

docs.title: 5.1 Types of Reports
docs.text:
In the Tandem Source Platform, you can find the reports when you tap the report section on the left sidebar.

Tandem Source offers three standard reports:

  • Overview which provides a general overview of your therapy data,

  • Daily Timeline that shows glucose readings, basal insulin delivery, and boluses day by day

  • and Pump Settings that includes the Displays pump settings, including all Personal Profiles and predictive technology usage (depending on your pump software),

Overview Report: The Overview report offers a summary of your pump and therapy data. The following summary information may be included in the default Overview report:.

  • Summary of CGM usage (e.g., average glucose reading, total time in target range, total time of use). If you do not have CGM data, BG entries on the pump appear instead.

  • Time in Range comparison between the current selected time period and the previous time period.

  • Summary of predictive technology usage (depending on your pump software).

  • A glucose trends graph showing a summary of glucose values during the selected period, including the median and interquartile range. If you do not have CGM data, the glucose trends graph is not displayed.

  • Summary of average daily insulin dosage, such as total insulin delivered, basal insulin delivered, and boluses delivered.

The second report from the Tandem Source platform is the Daily Timeline Report:

The Daily Timeline report shows glucose readings, basal and bolus insulin delivered, insulin suspension events, and other pump and therapy-related events that occurred during the selected date range.

The default view includes a key that defines crucial icons. Click Key to collapse the key.

The 3rd report is the Pump Settings report. This includes Personal Profiles for the most recent pump upload associated with your account. This report is read-only and cannot be used to change any settings in the pump itself. Pump settings shown on this report may include:

  • Profiles used during the selected time period

  • Tiles describing individual pump settings, including predictive technology settings (depending on your pump software), alert and alarm settings, and insulin delivery settings.

Different areas within Tandem Source allow you to open the Print screen and save or print any report:

Once the Print screen appears, select the desired date range and reports in the Select Reports window, then click Save Reports or Print Reports.

docs.title: 5.2 Interpreting reports
docs.text:
When interpreting reports with Tandem Control-IQ technology, the primary focus lies on optimizing the basal insulin rate, correction factor, and carbohydrate ratio, as these are crucial adjustable parameters. However, it's important to recognize that there is always room for improvement in the user's interaction with the pump.

This includes factors such as proper utilization of features like sleep mode and activity settings, adherence to bolus recommendations, and effectively managing infusion set changes. By continuously evaluating and refining these aspects, users can further enhance their experience and outcomes with Tandem Control-IQ technology.

Here's a roadmap for interpreting the reports:

1. Assess Glycemic Information: Begin by evaluating the TIR (Time in Range) and TBR (Time Below Range) over the past 2-4 weeks on the Overview Report.

  • Ensure that treatment goals are being met, with TIR above 70% and TBR below 4%.

  • It is important to pay attention to the frequency of meal bolusing, which is also indicated in the Overview Report. On average, there should be around 3 to 4 boluses per day for meals. If you notice an unusually high number of boluses, such as 20 boluses per day, it may indicate that the user doesn’’t trust the system and is constantly interfering with manual correction boluses.

2. Optimize Closed-Loop Settings: Analyze the AGP (Ambulatory Glucose Profile) on the Summary Tab to identify trends of hypo- or hyperglycemia.

Check if these trends are related to boluses on the Daily Timeline reports.

  • If you notice trends after meal boluses, it's important to assess various factors such as timing and accurate carbohydrate counting, before adjusting the carb ratio.

  • If you see a trend after manual or autocorrection boluses, you can try to adjust the correction factor.

  • And for trends outside of boluses, consider adjusting the basal insulin rate with 10-20% from 1 hour before the trend is noticed. Additionally, explore possible correlations with factors such as exercise, alcohol consumption, or hypoglycemia. Remember that with the Tandem Mobi Pump, it is possible to create a separate personal profile for special circumstances such as sports and illness.

In Tandem Control-IQ technology, it is important to ensure that the basal insulin rate is appropriately set. If you notice a consistent pattern of increasing or decreasing basal insulin during a specific period of the day the aim is to adjust the basal insulin rate at that time to align with the user's true basal insulin requirements. Keep in mind that it is generally recommended to aim for a basal bolus ratio between 40/60 and 60/40 %.

3. Guide Behavioral Recommendations: To ensure the proper usage of the automated insulin delivery system, it is important to review various aspects in the reports available. Here are some key considerations:

  • Check the sensor wear duration, time spent in auto mode, and adherence to the Sleep Activity. The sensor wear time and the time in automode should be more than 85%.

  • - The time in Sleep Activity should be approximately 8 hours a day or around 30% of the time. Ensure that a sleep schedule is set consistently to support effective glucose management during sleep.

  • Also in the Overview Report: check the amount of carbs entered for meals, evaluate if bolus calculator overrides are low (ideally 0%), and check the if the infusion set replacement is done every 3 days.

  • In the Daily Timeline Reports, you can assess the adherence to pre-meal bolusing, overcorrection of hypo- and hyperglycemia, and the appropriate use of Exercise and Sleep Activity.

  • And finally on the Pump Settings report, you can assess the appropriateness of alarm settings and reminders in the Tandem pump. Verify that the settings align with the user's needs and preferences.

4. Review Pump Settings and Emergency Plans:

  • Update weight and total daily dose of insulin at each check-up if necessary.

  • Document all pump settings and establish an emergency plan, including insulin pen usage, in case of a pump malfunction. Ensure access to insulin pens.

In conclusion, the accurate interpretation of reports and ongoing communication with your healthcare provider are essential for maximizing the benefits of Tandem Control-IQ technology. By effectively utilizing these tools and staying informed, you can enhance your diabetes management and lead a healthy lifestyle.

Remember, with the right tools and support, you can optimize your experience with Tandem Control-IQ technology and achieve better control over your diabetes. Stay proactive and empowered in your journey towards optimal health and well-being.

docs.title: 6. Managing special situations (4 min)
docs.text:
Welcome to this video on managing special situations with the Tandem Mobi System!

In this video, we will provide specific tips for managing hypo- and hyperglycemia, high-fat meals, sports, illness, alcohol and travel while using the Tandem Mobi System. These guidelines complement the general recommendations for managing these situations while wearing an automated insulin delivery system, which can be found in the general module on automated insulin delivery system.

docs.title: 6.1 Hypoglycemia
docs.text:
With the Tandem Mobi System, you may require fewer carbohydrates to treat or prevent hypoglycemia compared to when not using an AID system, especially if you have little insulin on board. You can check your insulin on board on the home screen.
